prompt:
I'm trying to get a sense of which HarFeast employees are most ready for the digital training rollout. Can you pull the workforce survey data and identify all employees who are above their role type's median readiness score, willing to pilot new tools, willing to spend >2 days in training with dedicated training time, and above the overall median digital comfort score?

Once you've identified that group, tell me:
1. How many "high-priority" employees are there, and what % of total employees do they represent?
2. How many total hours does this group spend weekly on manual entry, searching data, or fixing errors? What % of the company-wide total is that?
3. Break down the high-priority count by role type.

Report your answer here.
rubric: States that the number of high-priority employees is 57
States that the percentage of all employees the high-priority employees represent is 1.6%
States that the total hours high-priority employees spend on manual entry, searching data or fixing errors is 1259
States that the percentage of all such hours from high-priority employees is 1.8%
States that the number of high-priority employees in the Front-line role type is 23
States that the number of high-priority employees in the Back-office/Support role type is 23
States that the number of high-priority employees in the Supervisor/Team Lead role type is 5
States that the number of high-priority employees in the Management role type is 6

prompt:
Calculate the Adjusted Cost of Instability for each site, defined as Abnormal scrap cost/(Actual Scrap % - Normal Scrap %) = adjusted cost of instability. The target scrap rate of HarFeast is the minimum in the range of acceptable scrap rate in the scrap rate report. Just use COGS per ton as your scrap cost for now.

Report your final answers to me in a message. Round values to the nearest dollar.
rubric: States that the adjusted cost of instability for Bloomington, Illinois is $29,000,969,209
States that the adjusted cost of instability for Madison, Wisconsin is $26,298,130,974
States that the adjusted cost of instability for Cedar Rapids, Iowa is $25,277,106,218
States that the adjusted cost of instability for Cincinnati, Ohio is $27,899,188,122
States that the adjusted cost of instability for Kalamazoo, Michigan is $30,690,634,015

prompt:
Using HarFeast's equipment data, assess the impact of predictive maintenance on HarFeast's scrap rate. We will pilot predictive maintenance only on equipment a) whose scheduled hours per year are at or above that equipment type's median scheduled hours and b) whose labor hours are at or above its plant's median labor hours. For all equipment qualifying for the pilot, apply a 15% reduction to their scrap rate.

Calculate:
1. The new overall scrap rate for each product family (as a %)
2. The total number of scrap units each product family avoids every year

Report rounded to 1 decimal place for rates and nearest whole number for units.
rubric: States that the new overall scrap rate for Canned Vegetables is 6.3%
States that the scrap units Canned Vegetables avoids per year is 50900
States that the new overall scrap rate for Condiments is 5.9%
States that the scrap units Condiments avoids per year is 89848
States that the new overall scrap rate for Sauces is 6.2%
States that the scrap units Sauces avoids per year is 68682

task_name: Digital Lever Agreement and OEE Projections
prompt:
1. What is the digital lever that Sarah Jenkins, David Chen, and Mike Russo agree will deliver the fastest and biggest boost to HarFeast's Gross Margin?

2. Assuming HarFeast adopts the chosen digital lever, determine the OEE level in the first full year in each plant location where the annual OEE value exceeds the world-class target. Use the OEE improvement assumptions file for growth rates and start dates.

Report OEE values to 2 decimal places as percentages.
rubric: States that the digital lever is IoT Sensors for yield
States that the OEE level for Bloomington, Illinois in the first year exceeding world-class target is 85.75%
States that the first year Bloomington, Illinois exceeds world-class target is 2027
States that the OEE level for Madison, Wisconsin in the first year exceeding world-class target is 85.60%
States that the first year Madison, Wisconsin exceeds world-class target is 2028
States that the OEE level for Cedar Rapids, Iowa in the first year exceeding world-class target is 87.48%
States that the first year Cedar Rapids, Iowa exceeds world-class target is 2028
States that the OEE level for Cincinnati, Ohio in the first year exceeding world-class target is 85.94%
States that the first year Cincinnati, Ohio exceeds world-class target is 2031
States that the OEE level for Kalamazoo, Michigan in the first year exceeding world-class target is 86.22%
States that the first year Kalamazoo, Michigan exceeds world-class target is 2032

prompt:
1. Give me the total labor cost for each plant location (Bloomington, Illinois, Madison, Wisconsin, Cedar Rapids, Iowa only).

2. Give me the efficiency gains for each plant location. West North Central division plant locations only have a 10% annual efficiency gain from labor cost. For other locations, the efficiency gain is 20%. However, the efficiency gain is 5% for non-unionized production supervisors no matter where they are located.

3. Give me the forecasted labor cost increase from union demands, assuming a 5% increase for all union workers.

Round to the nearest dollar.
rubric: States that the Total Annual Labor Cost for Bloomington, Illinois is $760,406
States that the Efficiency Gains for Bloomington, Illinois is $97,288
States that the Union Demand Increase for Bloomington, Illinois is $13,947
States that the Total Annual Labor Cost for Madison, Wisconsin is $805,501
States that the Efficiency Gains for Madison, Wisconsin is $129,616
States that the Union Demand Increase for Madison, Wisconsin is $18,269
States that the Total Annual Labor Cost for Cedar Rapids, Iowa is $952,661
States that the Efficiency Gains for Cedar Rapids, Iowa is $92,565
States that the Union Demand Increase for Cedar Rapids, Iowa is $31,870

prompt:
Analyze the operational efficiency at HarFeast and assess how many inefficient employee hours each plant is recording on average. Which plants have the most efficient operations and the least efficient operations? How much more efficient are the highest efficiency locations vs the lowest efficiency locations?

Assume the following activities are considered inefficient: (a) manual data entry, (b) searching for data, (c) fixing errors. Use the workforce survey data. Report averages to 1 decimal place.
rubric: States the average inefficient time in Bloomington, Illinois is 8.5
States the average inefficient time in Madison, Wisconsin is 8.5
States the average inefficient time in Cedar Rapids, Iowa is 8.3
States the average inefficient time in Cincinnati, Ohio is 37.1
States the average inefficient time in Kalamazoo, Michigan is 37.9
States that Cedar Rapids, Iowa is a plant with the lowest average inefficient time
States that Kalamazoo, Michigan is the plant with the highest average inefficient time
States that the difference between highest and lowest average inefficient time is 357%

prompt:
I want to quantify the average annual productivity loss at a cost level for each employee in each primary role based on the sum of average hours spent doing manual entry, searching data, and fixing errors. Then, I want to calculate the total productivity loss cost HarFeast faces every year, company-wide.

Note that the survey responses represent one week of work. Report your final answer as a message. Round to the nearest dollar.
rubric: States the average annual productivity loss cost of a Production/Manufacturing Operator employee is $19,416
States the average annual productivity loss cost of a Quality Control/Quality Assurance employee is $23,654
States the average annual productivity loss cost of a Maintenance Technician employee is $27,272
States the average annual productivity loss cost of a Production Supervisor/Team Lead employee is $30,381
States the average annual productivity loss cost of a Supply Chain/Logistics Coordinator employee is $26,427
States the average annual productivity loss cost of a Demand Planning/Forecasting employee is $32,889
States the average annual productivity loss cost of a Administrative/Support Staff employee is $22,088
States the average annual productivity loss cost of a Plant Management employee is $43,952
States the total annual productivity loss cost is $98,517,379

prompt:
Using HarFeast's equipment data and quality losses dataset, consider all canned vegetables assets with a scrap rate > 5% and with unplanned downtime hours above the plant median for canned vegetables as "high-priority".

1. For the "high-priority" group, calculate the total annual quality-related losses (scrap cost + unplanned failure cost).
2. What percentage of all canned-vegetable quality losses comes from these high-priority assets?

Report losses rounded to the nearest dollar and percentage to the nearest whole number.
rubric: States that the total annual quality-related losses for the high-priority group is $1,201,893
States that the percentage of all canned-vegetable quality losses from high-priority assets is 35%

prompt:
Calculate the total labor variance in hours (favorable should be positive) and dollars for the Illinois and Wisconsin plants (Bloomington, Illinois and Madison, Wisconsin). A positive variance means Total Actual Hours are less than Total Standard Hours. Use the median wage for All Occupations in the food manufacturing industry from the BLS wage benchmark file to convert from hours to dollars.

Also give me the straight productivity index (Actual Hours / Standard Hours) for each plant.

Round hours to 2 decimal places, dollars to 2 decimal places, and the index to 2 decimal places.
rubric: States that the Labor Efficiency Variance (Hours) for Bloomington, Illinois is 19304 hours
States that the Labor Cost Variance for Bloomington, Illinois is $395152.88
States that the Productivity Index for Bloomington, Illinois is 0.88
States that the Labor Efficiency Variance (Hours) for Madison, Wisconsin is 13490 hours
States that the Labor Cost Variance for Madison, Wisconsin is $276140.3
States that the Productivity Index for Madison, Wisconsin is 0.92

task_name: Updated Productivity Loss with New Wages
prompt:
The client sent us employee wage data (attached), so we need to update our assumptions. Find the average hourly salary across all employee roles in the attached wage file and use that to calculate the updated annual productivity loss for the entire company.

Note that survey responses represent one week of work. Report the annual productivity loss in thousands (000s) rounded to the nearest thousand. Also state the average hourly wage used.

Report your answer here.
rubric: States the updated annual productivity loss is $115,371,000
States the average fully-loaded hourly wage is $31.65

prompt:
Identify the top five technology investments from the Aptean report with the largest positive difference in percentage revenue growth between users and non-users. Include only investments that the report explicitly identifies as either top technology investments to date or top investments planned for 2024.

Next, assume that HarFeast will deploy all five of these top initiatives at every plant location. Apply the cumulative growth impact to each plant's current unit sales and calculate the revised projected sales revenue.

Round unit sales to the nearest whole number and revenue to the nearest dollar.
rubric: States that the unit sales for Bloomington, Illinois after deploying initiatives is 23,433,911
States that the Revised Projected Sales for Bloomington, Illinois is $71,942,107
States that the unit sales for Madison, Wisconsin after deploying initiatives is 27,908,800
States that the Revised Projected Sales for Madison, Wisconsin is $85,680,016
States that the unit sales for Cedar Rapids, Iowa after deploying initiatives is 6,187,716
States that the Revised Projected Sales for Cedar Rapids, Iowa is $36,012,507
States that the unit sales for Cincinnati, Ohio after deploying initiatives is 7,087,630
States that the Revised Projected Sales for Cincinnati, Ohio is $47,416,245
States that the unit sales for Kalamazoo, Michigan after deploying initiatives is 8,133,334
States that the Revised Projected Sales for Kalamazoo, Michigan is $51,158,671

prompt:
To implement the required roadmap, we need to identify what roles and plants are most and least willing to go through a digital transformation.

Determine the plant with the highest and lowest average willingness to adopt digital tools. Within those plants, identify the roles with the highest and lowest willingness. For those specific role-plant combinations, determine the preferred training length, the count of employees preferring 1-2 days of training, and the total training cost (at $8/hour training rate).

Report your findings here.
rubric: States that the plant with lowest willingness to adopt is Bloomington, Illinois
States that the plant with highest willingness to adopt is Madison, Wisconsin
States the role with lowest willingness in Bloomington, Illinois is Demand Planning/Forecasting
States the role with highest willingness in Madison, Wisconsin is Production/Manufacturing Operator

prompt:
Can you look at the Frito-Lay case study and apply their downtime reduction to HarFeast's numbers in the equipment data? I want to estimate what the improvement would look like for us (rounded to the nearest full percentage point).

Calculate the current unplanned downtime ratio (unplanned downtime hours / scheduled hours) for each plant, apply the reduction from the case study, and report the new ratios.

Output the information in a message here.
rubric: States that the new unplanned downtime ratio for Bloomington, Illinois is 6%
States that the new unplanned downtime ratio for Madison, Wisconsin is 6%
States that the new unplanned downtime ratio for Cedar Rapids, Iowa is 6%
States that the new unplanned downtime ratio for Cincinnati, Ohio is 6%
States that the new unplanned downtime ratio for Kalamazoo, Michigan is 6%

prompt:
Use the workforce survey responses to identify the number of respondents who received any kind of training on digital tools. Of those respondents, return the percentage of respondents for each training quality rating.

Reply back here to me.
rubric: States that the number of respondents who received training is 1462
States that percentage of respondents rated training as "Excellent- comprehensive and very helpful" is 17%
States that percentage of respondents rated training as "Good- adequate for most needs" is 42%
States that percentage of respondents rated training as "Fair- some gaps or inconsistencies" is 32%
